pattern: Each element scores a point for a contiguous range of rotation amounts, so marking the start and end of every element's scoring interval on a difference array and prefix-summing reveals the rotation with the most points in linear time.
hints: Recomputing the whole score for every rotation is quadratic; instead ask, for a single element, exactly which rotations let it earn its point.

An element at original index i lands on index (i-k) mod n after rotating by k, and it scores when that landing index is at least its value; that condition holds for a contiguous (wrap-around) band of k values.

For each i compute the rotation interval where it scores, add +1 at the interval start and -1 just past its end on a length-n difference array (splitting wrap-around into two pieces), prefix-sum to get each rotation's score, and return the smallest argmax.

edge_inputs.description: best rotation is in the middle
every rotation ties so the smallest index wins
all zeros, every position always scores
values equal to their indices already, rotation zero is strong
descending values reward a particular shift

stress.description: random arrays with each value below the length so all are valid
stress.note: value < length matches the constraint 0 <= nums[i] < n
